name: Ubuntu
description: Ubuntu is a complete desktop Linux operating system, freely available with both community and professional support. It is the most popular Linux distribution for beginners.
weaknesses: Default GNOME may not suit all users
Snaps can be slower than native packages

name: Kubuntu
description: Kubuntu is an official Ubuntu flavor featuring the KDE Plasma desktop environment. It provides a modern, customizable interface with Ubuntu's stability.

name: Ubuntu MATE
description: Ubuntu MATE is an official Ubuntu flavor featuring the MATE desktop environment, designed for stability and traditional desktop experience.

name: Ubuntu Budgie
description: Ubuntu Budgie is an official Ubuntu flavor featuring the Budgie desktop environment, providing a simple and elegant desktop experience.

name: Ubuntu Studio
description: Ubuntu Studio is an official Ubuntu flavor optimized for audio, video, and graphics production with pre-installed creative software.

name: Linux Mint
description: Linux Mint is a Ubuntu-based distribution which provides a complete out-of-the-box experience with browser plugins, media codecs, and custom configuration tools.
weaknesses: Based on Ubuntu (inherits Ubuntu's choices), Less cutting-edge software

name: Zorin OS
description: Zorin OS is a beginner-friendly, Ubuntu-based desktop distribution focused on familiarity and polish with Windows-like and macOS-like desktop layouts.

name: Pop!_OS
description: Pop!_OS is an Ubuntu-based Linux distribution featuring a custom GNOME desktop developed by System76, optimized for their hardware but available for all.

name: elementary OS
description: elementary OS is a design-forward distribution built on Ubuntu LTS with the Pantheon desktop, featuring a curated AppCenter and privacy-respecting defaults.

name: Fedora
description: Fedora is a cutting-edge Linux distribution sponsored by Red Hat, featuring the latest software and serving as a preview for RHEL features.

name: Debian
description: Debian is the universal operating system, known for its stability, security, and massive software repository. It's the base for Ubuntu and many other distros.

name: Arch Linux
description: Arch Linux is a lightweight and flexible Linux distribution that tries to Keep It Simple. It provides a minimal base system and lets users build their system from the ground up.
weaknesses: Can break if not updated carefully

name: Manjaro
description: Manjaro is a user-friendly Linux distribution based on Arch Linux. It provides all the benefits of Arch Linux with an easier installation and more user-friendly approach.

name: Kali Linux
description: Kali Linux is a Debian-based Linux distribution designed for digital forensics and penetration testing. It comes pre-installed with hundreds of tools for security testing.

name: Lubuntu
description: Lubuntu is a lightweight Linux distribution using the LXQt desktop environment. It focuses on speed and energy efficiency, making it ideal for older hardware.

name: Xubuntu
description: Xubuntu is a community-developed Ubuntu variant using the XFCE desktop environment. It provides a stable, light, and configurable desktop environment.

name: openSUSE Tumbleweed
description: openSUSE Tumbleweed is a rolling-release distribution with the latest software. It uses the Btrfs filesystem with snapshot capabilities for system rollback.

name: openSUSE Leap
description: openSUSE Leap is a stable distribution that shares binary packages with SUSE Linux Enterprise. It's ideal for servers and enterprise use.

name: MX Linux
description: MX Linux is a mid-weight Debian-based distribution featuring a custom MX Tools suite for system management and configuration.

name: Deepin
description: Deepin is a Debian-based distribution featuring the Deepin Desktop Environment (DDE), designed with a macOS-like aesthetic and user experience.

name: Peppermint OS
description: Peppermint OS is a lightweight Debian-based distribution using the XFCE desktop, optimized for speed and cloud applications.

name: Linux Lite
description: Linux Lite is a beginner-friendly Ubuntu-based distribution designed to introduce Windows users to Linux.

name: antiX
description: antiX is a lightweight Debian-based distribution designed for older computers, featuring antiX Magic for system configuration.

name: EndeavourOS
description: EndeavourOS is an Arch-based distribution with a graphical installer, providing an easy way to experience Arch Linux with multiple desktop options.

name: Garuda Linux
description: Garuda Linux is a performance-oriented Arch-based distribution featuring the Chalet-AUR helper, gaming optimizations, and beautiful themes.

name: ArcoLinux
description: ArcoLinux is an Arch-based distribution with a focus on customization and education, featuring many desktop editions and extensive documentation.

name: Fedora KDE
description: Fedora KDE Spin is an official Fedora edition featuring the KDE Plasma desktop, providing a modern and customizable experience with Fedora's cutting-edge software.

name: Fedora XFCE
description: Fedora XFCE Spin is a lightweight Fedora edition featuring the XFCE desktop, ideal for older hardware and users who prefer a traditional desktop.

name: Nobara Linux
description: Nobara Linux is a Fedora-based distribution optimized for gaming and content creation, with gaming tweaks and pre-installed software.

name: Red Hat Enterprise Linux
description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux is a commercial Linux distribution for enterprise servers, with long-term support and certification for critical workloads.

name: Rocky Linux
description: Rocky Linux is a community-driven enterprise Linux distribution, binary-compatible with RHEL, created as a CentOS replacement.

name: AlmaLinux
description: AlmaLinux is a community-driven enterprise Linux distribution, binary-compatible with RHEL, created as a CentOS replacement with a non-profit governance model.

name: CentOS Stream
description: CentOS Stream is a rolling-release preview of what's coming in RHEL, serving as the upstream for future RHEL releases.

name: Solus
description: Solus is an independently developed distribution featuring the Budgie desktop, with a curated software repository and rolling updates.

name: NixOS
description: NixOS is a Linux distribution built on the Nix package manager, featuring declarative system configuration and atomic upgrades.

name: Gentoo
description: Gentoo is a source-based distribution known for its flexibility and performance optimization through compile-time configuration.

name: Slackware
description: Slackware is one of the oldest Linux distributions, known for its simplicity, stability, and adherence to Unix philosophy.

name: Void Linux
description: Void Linux is an independent distribution with a binary package system and optional source builds, featuring runit init system.

name: Alpine Linux
description: Alpine Linux is a security-oriented, lightweight Linux distribution designed for power users who appreciate security, simplicity and resource efficiency.

name: Tails
description: Tails is a live operating system that routes all internet traffic through Tor and leaves no trace on the computer unless explicitly asked.

name: Qubes OS
description: Qubes OS is a security-focused desktop operating system that uses compartmentalization through Xen-based virtual machines.

name: Parrot OS
description: Parrot OS is a Debian-based security-focused distribution designed for penetration testing, cryptography, and privacy.
weaknesses: Not as many tools as Kali

name: KDE neon
description: KDE neon is an Ubuntu-based distribution featuring the latest KDE Plasma desktop and KDE applications, built by the KDE team.

name: Trisquel
description: Trisquel is a 100% free software Ubuntu-based distribution that removes all proprietary software and firmware.

name: PureOS
description: PureOS is a Debian-based distribution focused on privacy and free software, developed by Purism for their Librem laptops.

name: SparkyLinux
description: SparkyLinux is a lightweight Debian-based distribution available in multiple editions, with rolling and semi-rolling options.

name: Artix Linux
description: Artix Linux is an Arch-based distribution that replaces systemd with OpenRC, runit, or s6 init systems for traditional Unix experience.

name: ROSA Linux
description: ROSA Linux is a Russian Linux distribution based on RPM, featuring the ROSA Fresh platform with long-term support.

name: Puppy Linux
description: Puppy Linux is a family of lightweight Linux distributions designed for older computers, running entirely in RAM.

name: Tiny Core Linux
description: Tiny Core Linux is an extremely lightweight Linux distribution with a 16MB core, designed for maximum minimalism.

name: Bazzite
description: Bazzite is a Fedora Atomic-based distribution optimized for gaming and handheld PCs, featuring atomic updates, rollback capabilities, and SteamOS-like experience.

name: Bluefin
description: Bluefin is a cloud-native Fedora Atomic-based workstation designed for developers, featuring immutable system with atomic updates and container-based development workflow.

name: Vanilla OS
description: Vanilla OS is an Ubuntu-based distribution with an immutable root filesystem using ABroot, providing atomic updates and rollback functionality with a pure GNOME experience.

name: CachyOS
description: CachyOS is an Arch-based distribution optimized for performance with gaming tweaks, custom kernels, and multiple desktop environments including Wayland compositors.

name: BigLinux
description: BigLinux is a Brazilian Ubuntu-based distribution featuring the BigDE desktop environment with a focus on ease of use and Brazilian localization.

name: Reborn OS
description: Reborn OS is an Arch-based distribution with a graphical installer and multiple desktop options, featuring the RebornOS repository for additional software.

name: Raspberry Pi OS
description: Raspberry Pi OS is the official operating system for Raspberry Pi computers, optimized for ARM hardware with beginner-friendly tools and extensive documentation.

name: Nitrux
description: Nitrux is a Debian-based distribution featuring KDE Plasma, focusing on AppImage-first software delivery and a modular approach without systemd.

name: Bedrock Linux
description: Bedrock Linux is a meta-distribution that allows users to combine features from multiple distributions, running software from various package managers simultaneously.

name: Clear Linux OS
description: Clear Linux OS is an Intel-optimized rolling distribution focused on performance, security, and cloud-native computing with minimal footprint.

name: SliTaz
description: SliTaz is an extremely lightweight distribution (~30MB) focused on speed, stability, and security, suitable for very old hardware.

name: Kodachi
description: Kodachi is a privacy-focused live OS with built-in VPN, Tor, encryption tools, and security hardening for anonymous browsing.

name: OpenMediaVault
description: OpenMediaVault is a Debian-based network-attached storage (NAS) solution with a web-based management interface for file sharing and media serving.
